image.alt: Black-and-white photograph of a moustached man in a formal dark Western suit and tie

rounds.revealFact: Sun Yat-sen helped topple China's last dynasty in 1911 and is honored on both sides of the Taiwan Strait as a founding father of modern China.

image.alt: Painting of charging highland clansmen clashing with a line of red-coated infantry

clues: Lightly-armed clansmen charged across open, boggy moorland straight into disciplined musket volleys and cannon fire.
The slaughter ended a dynasty's last bid to reclaim a throne and broke an old way of life for good.

pairs.a: George Eastman introduces the handheld Kodak camera
pairs.b: Louis Daguerre unveils the daguerreotype, making photography practical

pairs.a: Eadweard Muybridge's photos prove a galloping horse fully leaves the ground
pairs.b: The Lumière brothers hold the first public movie screening

pairs.a: Technicolor fills a full-length feature with vivid color
pairs.b: The Jazz Singer brings synchronized speech to the movies

pairs.a: Walt Disney releases the first feature-length animated film
pairs.b: Color television reaches American living rooms

pairs.a: The first camera-phone picture is shared
pairs.b: Kodachrome color film reaches everyday photographers

image.alt: Aerial color photo of a large curved concrete dam with round intake towers, a reservoir on one side and a river canyon on the other

attribution.fileTitle: File:HOOVER DAM ON THE COLORADO RIVER - NARA - 548938.jpg
attribution.author: Charles O'Rear, U.S. National Archives (DOCUMERICA)

followUpQuestion.prompt: This dam holds back which river?

rounds.prompt: Colonists in Boston dump chests of British tea into the harbor to protest taxation.
rounds.revealFact: The Boston Tea Party, a protest against British taxes, hardened tensions that soon boiled over into the American Revolution.

rounds.prompt: Where did an unknown civilization lay out a huge grid-planned city crowned by vast pyramids to the Sun and Moon?
rounds.revealFact: Teotihuacan was one of the largest cities of the ancient world, already a vast ruin when the later Aztecs found it and gave it the name we still use.
